prompt:
Add WAYLAND_DISPLAY to tmux's update-environment so Wayland-aware tools (like wl-paste) work correctly inside tmux panes on NixOS.

File: home/common.nix
Location: lines 128-142, inside programs.tmux.extraConfig

Current extraConfig block:

    extraConfig = ''
      set-option -g status-style fg=white,bg=colour23
      set -g base-index 1
      set-window-option -g mode-keys emacs
      unbind-key C-b
    '';

Change: append this line inside the extraConfig string (after 'unbind-key C-b'):

      set-option -ga update-environment ' WAYLAND_DISPLAY'

Note the '-ga' flag (append) and the leading space inside the quoted string — tmux's update-environment is a space-separated list, so we append with a leading space to avoid concatenating with the previous entry.

Why: on Wayland systems, WAYLAND_DISPLAY is not in tmux's default update-environment list, so new shells spawned inside tmux have an empty WAYLAND_DISPLAY. This breaks clipboard tools like wl-paste/wl-copy that need to know which Wayland socket to use. The user runs NixOS on Wayland and noticed wl-paste failing to read the clipboard inside tmux.

Acceptance criteria:
- home/common.nix modified with the one-line addition
- Verify the flake still evaluates: run 'nix flake check' or at minimum 'nix eval .#homeConfigurations --apply builtins.attrNames' to make sure the config parses. If there's a more appropriate check for this repo, use that.
- Do not modify any other files or configuration
- PR title: 'tmux: pass WAYLAND_DISPLAY into tmux env'
- PR body should briefly explain the why (wl-paste/clipboard tools inside tmux on Wayland)

Do not kill the user's running tmux server or rebuild the system — that's on the user to do after merging.
